fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 28-year-old African American male with schizoaffective disorder for routine check.
fields.Subjective: Mr. Mitchell came to appointment on time appearing upbeat and talkative. Reports consistent adherence to brexpiprazole 3 mg every morning taken with breakfast, never missed a dose since starting six months ago. States feeling much improved compared to before medication. Experiences very occasional brief visual distortions maybe once a month which don't bother him. No auditory hallucinations. No paranoid thoughts. Sleep consistent at 7 hours nightly. Appetite good, weight stable at 172 lbs. Working as warehouse associate, enjoys physical work and team. Mood 'positive' rated 8/10. Lives in shared apartment with two roommates. Plays basketball recreationally. Attending community college part-time studying business. One year clean from all substances. Building new friendships. Future goals include management position. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Seen by Jamal Washington, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Athletic build, casual athletic wear, excellent grooming, energetic
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Future-oriented, minimal residual symptoms, good insight

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizoaffective Disorder, currently stable with excellent response to treatment.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Living with roommates; employed full-time; attending college part-time; one year sober; active recreational and social life.
Assessment_and_Plan: Excellent medication adherence with strong clinical response.
Commended on employment, education, and recovery.
fields.Author: Note written by Jamal Washington, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner — Urban Community Health, 5521 Warehouse District, Detroit, MI 48201, Phone: (555) 338-9921, Fax: (555) 338-9922
